evals.name: Trigger: Meter/Gauge Reading
evals.description: Verify skill activates for specialized display reading
evals.prompt: How do I read the numbers from a digital meter display in MATLAB?
evals.expected_output: Agent activates matlab-ocr skill
assertions: Agent activates matlab-ocr skill

evals.name: Trigger: Image Classification (Negative)
evals.description: Skill should NOT follow OCR workflow for general image classification
evals.prompt: Classify objects in an image using a pretrained neural network in MATLAB
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT follow OCR workflow — no binarization, no ocr() call, no OCR Plan
assertions: Agent does not follow OCR workflow for this task (no ocr() call, no OCR Plan heading, no binarization step)

evals.name: Negative trigger: handwriting recognition
evals.description: Tests that cursive handwriting is correctly identified as outside OCR capability
evals.prompt: I want to recognize cursive handwriting in scanned letters using MATLAB
evals.expected_output: Agent warns that cursive handwriting is not supported by MATLAB OCR
assertions: Response contains 'cursive' or 'handwriting' with a warning or limitation statement
Does not contain a full 6-step OCR pipeline for handwriting
Response mentions that OCR engine is trained on printed characters or similar limitation

evals.name: Basic Document OCR
evals.description: Read text from a clean business card image — tests diagnostic workflow
evals.prompt: I have a business card image at 'businessCard.png'. Read all the text from it using MATLAB.
evals.expected_output: Starts with diagnostic step, uses ocr(), displays results
assertions: Uses ocr function
Displays or returns recognized text
Agent writes an ocr_pipeline_*.m file to disk
Agent writes a decision_log.txt file to disk

evals.name: Plan announced before MATLAB execution
evals.description: Tests Critical Rule 1: agent must output OCR Plan before any MATLAB code execution
evals.prompt: I have a photo of stamped metal text. Before writing any MATLAB code, show me your OCR Plan for how you would approach extracting the text.
evals.expected_output: Agent outputs a visual characterization and OCR Plan block BEFORE any MATLAB code
assertions: Response contains '## OCR Plan' or 'OCR Plan' heading before any MATLAB code block
Response contains 'Difficulty' or 'Strategy' fields in the plan block
Response does not contain ```matlab code before the 'OCR Plan' text appears

evals.name: Low Contrast / Uneven Lighting
evals.description: Fix OCR on image with non-uniform illumination
evals.prompt: I'm trying to read text from a photo of a keypad but ocr() returns garbage. The lighting is uneven across the image. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Applies illumination correction preprocessing before OCR
assertions: Applies preprocessing to correct uneven illumination (imtophat, adapthisteq, or similar)
Uses ocr function after preprocessing

evals.name: Text on textured background uses SAM segmentation
evals.description: Tests Critical Rule 4: text overlaid ON texture uses imsegsam, not local contrast
evals.prompt: I have a photo of a printed label stuck on a wooden crate. The wood grain texture makes OCR fail. What segmentation approach should I use to isolate the label before running OCR? Just explain the approach, don't run code.
evals.expected_output: Agent recommends imsegsam since the label is a separate object on a textured background
assertions: Response recommends imsegsam or SAM (Segment Anything Model) as the primary segmentation approach
Does not recommend imgaussfilt background subtraction as the primary approach for this scenario
Response mentions the support package requirement (Image Processing Toolbox Automated Visual Inspection Library)

evals.name: detectTextCRAFT is default for scene text
evals.description: Tests Critical Rule 5: CRAFT is the default text detection method for natural scenes
evals.prompt: I have a photo of a product package on a store shelf. What text detection method should I use before calling ocr() in MATLAB? Just explain the approach, don't run code.
evals.expected_output: Agent recommends detectTextCRAFT as the default for scene text
assertions: Response mentions 'detectTextCRAFT' or 'CRAFT' as the recommended detection method for this scene
Does not recommend 'detectMSERFeatures' as the PRIMARY detection method (mentioning it as a fallback is acceptable)

evals.name: Multi-Language OCR (Japanese)
evals.description: Read Japanese text from a scanned document
evals.prompt: I have a scanned Japanese document. How do I extract the text using MATLAB OCR?
evals.expected_output: Uses Model='japanese', mentions support package
assertions: Uses Model="japanese" parameter syntax
Does not use deprecated Language= parameter
Decision log contains add-on check result for OCR Language Data (installed or missing)

evals.name: Empty Results Troubleshooting (Polarity)
evals.description: Troubleshoot empty OCR results from inverted polarity
evals.prompt: My ocr() call returns empty text (results.Text is blank). The image clearly has white text on a dark background. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Identifies polarity as issue, shows imcomplement fix
assertions: Uses imcomplement as the fix
Mentions polarity or that OCR expects dark text on light background
Provides code example showing imcomplement usage

evals.name: Receipt Scanning with Mixed Content
evals.description: Extract prices and item names from a receipt photo
evals.prompt: I photographed a receipt and want to extract the item names and prices. The receipt has mixed text and numbers. How do I do this in MATLAB?
evals.expected_output: Preprocessing pipeline, LayoutAnalysis, confidence or validation step
assertions: Uses ocr function
Includes a validation or filtering step (confidence, pattern matching, or post-processing)
Agent writes an ocr_pipeline_*.m file to disk
Agent writes a decision_log.txt file to disk

evals.name: CharacterSet used for known-domain OCR
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses CharacterSet to constrain recognition when domain is known
evals.prompt: I need to read serial numbers from industrial parts. The serial numbers only contain uppercase letters and digits (no lowercase, no special characters). Show me the key ocr() call with the right parameters to constrain recognition. Just explain the approach, don't run code.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses CharacterSet parameter to constrain to uppercase + digits
assertions: Response contains 'CharacterSet' parameter in an ocr() call
CharacterSet value includes digits (0-9) and uppercase letters (A-Z)
Response mentions that constraining characters reduces misreads like O/0 or l/1

evals.name: Batch OCR pipeline for multiple images
evals.description: Tests that the agent can structure a batch processing pipeline for a folder of images
evals.prompt: I have a folder of 200 receipt images at /data/receipts/. I need to extract text from all of them and save results to a CSV. How do I set up a batch OCR pipeline in MATLAB?
evals.expected_output: Agent creates a loop-based pipeline using imageDatastore or dir() to process multiple images
assertions: Generated code uses imageDatastore, dir, or fullfile for file discovery
Generated code contains a loop (for or parfor) to process multiple images
Generated code contains 'ocr' inside the processing loop
Response mentions saving results to a file (CSV, table, or writetable)

evals.name: Custom Model Training Assessment
evals.description: User asks about training custom OCR for proprietary industrial font
evals.prompt: Our factory has custom LED displays with a proprietary font. Standard OCR gives terrible results even after preprocessing. Should I train a custom model? How?
evals.expected_output: Assessment checklist, trainOCR workflow, ground truth, evaluateOCR
assertions: Includes assessment criteria or checklist before recommending training
Mentions trainOCR or ocrTrainer or ocrTrainingOptions or ocrTrainingData as the training approach
Mentions labeled data, ground truth, or Image Labeler

evals.name: Deskewing Rotated Text
evals.description: Correct skewed text before OCR on a real tilted document image
evals.prompt: Straighten and OCR the tilted document at evals/fixtures/tilted_document_5deg.png. Save as ocr_pipeline_tilted_doc.m with a decision_log.txt.
evals.expected_output: Detects skew angle and uses imrotate to correct, then runs OCR
assertions: Detects the skew angle (via hough, regionprops, or similar technique)
Uses imrotate or imwarp to correct the skew
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Agent writes an ocr_pipeline_*.m file to disk
Agent writes a decision_log.txt file to disk

evals.name: Small Text Scaling
evals.description: Scale up small text that OCR cannot read reliably
evals.prompt: OCR this tiny business card image at evals/fixtures/tiny_text_business_card.png. The text is about 10 pixels tall and ocr() returns almost nothing. Save the pipeline.
evals.expected_output: Uses imresize to scale up, recognizes MathWorks text, saves pipeline
assertions: Uses imresize to scale up the image
Mentions scaling factor (2x, 3x, 4x, or higher) or 300 DPI
Does not suggest only changing OCR parameters without resizing

evals.name: Regression: LayoutAnalysis with ROI
evals.description: Ensure LayoutAnalysis is always set when providing bounding boxes
evals.prompt: I used detectTextCRAFT to find bounding boxes around words in my street sign image. Now I want to pass each bounding box to ocr() to read the text. What's the correct syntax?
evals.expected_output: Uses ocr(I, bbox, LayoutAnalysis='Word') — always specifies LayoutAnalysis with bounding boxes
assertions: Sets LayoutAnalysis parameter when passing bounding boxes to ocr()
Does not show ocr(I, bbox) without LayoutAnalysis specified

evals.name: Regression: Confidence Does Not Guarantee Correctness
evals.description: Ensure skill warns that high confidence can accompany wrong results
evals.prompt: My OCR result has 0.96 confidence but the text looks wrong. Is high confidence reliable?
evals.expected_output: Agent warns that high confidence does not guarantee correctness, especially on small or unusual images
assertions: Warns that high confidence does not guarantee correct text
Does not state that 0.96 confidence means the result is reliable

evals.name: Regression: do not use SAM for stamped/engraved text
evals.description: Regression test for common error of using imsegsam on text formed by surface texture
evals.prompt: I have an image of serial numbers engraved into aluminum. The characters are cut into the metal surface. A colleague suggested using SAM segmentation (imsegsam) to isolate the stamped text before running OCR. Is this the right approach?
evals.expected_output: Agent corrects the user — SAM cannot isolate surface features. Uses local contrast subtraction instead.
assertions: Response explicitly states SAM or imsegsam cannot isolate stamped/engraved surface features
Response contains 'imgaussfilt' or 'local contrast subtraction' as the recommended approach
Response explains that SAM segments objects not surface features

evals.name: Regression: do not skip Step 1 diagnosis
evals.description: Regression test for the anti-pattern of jumping straight to code without diagnosis. Known limitation: agent may comply with explicit skip requests.
evals.prompt: I have a scanned document image. Just quickly run ocr() on it, skip the analysis and preprocessing steps, I just want fast results. Don't bother with the planning step.
evals.expected_output: Agent explains why diagnosis cannot be skipped and performs at minimum a brief characterization before proceeding
assertions: Response contains image characterization, explains why diagnosis matters, OR acknowledges the user's skip request before proceeding
Response does not blindly run ocr() without any comment about the skipped diagnosis — it either diagnoses first or explains why it is proceeding directly

evals.name: evaluateOCR used with correct signature
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses evaluateOCR with the correct argument types (ocrText object + cell array of ground truth strings)
evals.prompt: I ran ocr() on a scanned receipt and got results. The receipt should say 'TOTAL $42.99' and 'THANK YOU'. How do I compute Character Error Rate and Word Error Rate to measure accuracy? Just show the key call, don't run code.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses evaluateOCR(results, {"TOTAL $42.99"; "THANK YOU"}) with the ocrText object as first arg and a cell array as second
assertions: Response contains 'evaluateOCR' function call
First argument to evaluateOCR is the ocrText results object (not raw text strings)
Second argument is a cell array or string array of ground truth text (not a single string)

evals.name: Agent asks user when confidence is low
evals.description: Tests that the agent pauses and asks the user for ground truth when OCR confidence is below threshold, rather than silently presenting bad results
evals.prompt: I ran this OCR pipeline and got: results.Text = 'Mcciivj a 3pn Tuarlay', mean word confidence 0.42. The image is a handwritten note. What should I do next?
evals.expected_output: Agent flags the low confidence, identifies handwriting as a limitation, and asks the user to confirm or provide the correct text rather than trying more preprocessing
assertions: Response acknowledges low confidence (mentions 0.42 or 'below threshold' or similar)
Response asks the user what the text actually says OR states that handwriting is outside OCR capability
Response does not recommend trying 5+ additional preprocessing approaches without user input
